name: Clark, Kyle
resolved_employer: University of Texas (per filing; possible match to Kyle Clark, Assistant Dean & Director of Student Support, Moody College of Communication, UT Austin — unconfirmed)
evidence: Filing lists occupation 'Educator' and employer 'University of Texas', which supports an Education industry classification on employer fallback alone. A plausible candidate exists — Kyle Clark, Assistant Dean and Director of Student Support at UT Austin's Moody College of Communication — but nothing in the public record ties him to the Round Rock 78664 zip on the filing, and 'Kyle Clark' is a very common name (the fec_donation_count of 45 is almost certainly aggregating multiple people, including the well-known Denver news anchor of the same name). Identity not confirmed; industry recorded as employer fallback only.

name: Longoria, Isabel
resolved_employer: Annie's List — Executive Director (Texas PAC that recruits, trains and elects progressive women candidates); formerly Harris County Elections Administrator
evidence: Confirmed as the notable Isabel Longoria: Houston-based (matches 77022), listed by name and title as Executive Director on Annie's List's own About page, which matches the filing's occupation 'Director' / employer "Annie's List". She was Harris County's first Elections Administrator (sworn in Nov. 2020, resigned effective July 1, 2022) and previously ran for Houston City Council District H in 2019. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found; no ideological-PAC federal contributions surfaced.

affiliations.snippet: Annie's List About page lists Isabel Longoria as Executive Director; the organization's stated mission is to 'achieve equity for women by changing the face of power in Texas' by recruiting, training, supporting and electing progressive women.
Isabel Longoria was sworn in as Harris County's first-ever elections administrator on Nov. 18, 2020, after serving as special adviser on voting rights to County Clerk Chris Hollins.

name: Moon, Amber
resolved_employer: Meta Platforms, Inc. — Public Affairs Manager (since Feb. 2020)
evidence: Confirmed as the Washington, DC-area public affairs professional (Annandale VA 22003 is a DC suburb): LegiStorm records her as Public Affairs Manager at Meta Platforms Inc. since February 2020, matching the filing's occupation 'Public Affairs' / employer 'Meta, Inc.' Career history is Democratic communications: Communications Director and official spokesperson for U.S. Sen. Gary Peters (D-MI), and — per her LinkedIn — prior roles at the DCCC and the Texas Democratic Party. Between the Senate and Meta she was Director of External Communications at defense contractor BAE Systems, Inc., where she was the named media contact on corporate press releases in Dec. 2019. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration and no registered federal lobbying found; no ideological-PAC federal contributions surfaced.

affiliations.snippet: BAE Systems, Inc. press release of Dec. 17, 2019 lists Amber Moon (amber.moon@baesystems.com, 703-312-6195) as the company's media contact. BAE Systems is a major defense contractor.
LegiStorm profile: 'Amber Moon - Meta Platforms Inc. (Feb. 2020-), Public Affairs Manager.'

affiliations.org: Office of U.S. Senator Gary Peters (D-MI)
affiliations.snippet: University of Houston alumni profile: Amber Moon (BS Political Science, 2003) is 'Communications Director' for 'U.S. Senator Gary Peters,' serving as the Senator's official spokesperson.

name: Jammer, Brian

evidence: The filing carried no employer, but the public record resolves him firmly. The City of Austin lobbyist registry lists 'Jammer, Brian' as a lobbying CLIENT — a property owner at 1197 San Bernard Street, Austin, TX 78702 — an exact match to the donor's 78702 zip. The same Brian K. Jammer is an Austin attorney (UT Law 1992) practicing government, election/political and health care law, and serves as VP of State Government Relations in Baylor College of Medicine's Office of the General Counsel; he appears on Texas Ethics Commission registered-lobbyist lists for 2020, 2022, 2024 and 2026. Notably, this donor is both a registered state lobbyist and a party that has retained Austin land-use lobbyists on his own behalf before the City.

name: Teich, Ann
resolved_employer: Austin ISD (retired) — language arts teacher at Porter Middle School for 27 years, then AISD substitute teacher; former AISD Board of Trustees member, District 3 (2012-2020)
evidence: Ann Teich taught language arts in Austin ISD for 27 years at Porter Middle School and was elected to the AISD Board of Trustees for District 3 in November 2012, re-elected in 2016, leaving office in 2020. District 3 covers Central/North Austin (Mueller and Hyde Park up to the Domain), which matches the donor's North Austin zip 78758; the filing's 'Not Employed' occupation matches her retired-teacher status. Identity corroborated on district geography, zip, and retired-educator occupation. Secondary reports of additional civic board seats (North Austin Civic Association, Foundation for the Homeless, Religious Coalition to Assist the Homeless, Downtown Cluster of Congregations) appeared in search results but could not be verified against a retrievable primary source, so they are not recorded here.

affiliations.role: Trustee, District 3 (elected Nov 2012; re-elected Nov 2016; left office 2020)
affiliations.snippet: Austin ISD's press release on the swearing-in of new trustees identifies Ann Teich as "a newly elected trustee who represents Central and North Austin in District 3." AISD's later board profile adds that she "was a language arts teacher in AISD for 27 years at Porter Middle School" before her election.

The notably Republican-leaning federal donation history (12 contributions, lean 0.18) could not be tied to any specific PAC or ideological committee through public search, and no affiliation is recorded on the basis of donation lean alone.
